summary: Iter77 E2E test of Careers Mini ATS. Backend 15/15 pytest PASS: settings GET/PUT auth-required, PUT valid+blank persists, PUT invalid email 400, apply invalid-email 400, apply success returns ok+application_id+email_notified(false expected), duplicate email 400, unknown slug 404, resume lands in Documents folder='Resumes', GET /careers/applications strips resume_b64, PATCH status valid/invalid, GET /careers/applications/{id}/resume returns filename+mime+decodable b64, GET /careers/jobs includes application_count. Frontend PUBLIC /careers apply flow WORKS: modal opens on job card click, apply-form renders, no-resume shows error toast 'Attach your resume (PDF/DOC, max 5 MB)', full submission with PDF resume shows apply-success 'Application received!'. Frontend ADMIN Job Postings: ats-notify-settings visible, ats-notify-email save fires success toast, application_count badge shows correctly (3) on job card. CRITICAL BUG: clicking job-applicants-{id} button does NOTHING because ApplicantsModal is defined at line 179 of JobPostings.jsx but is NEVER rendered — state 'applicants' is set via setApplicants(j) but no {applicants && <ApplicantsModal .../>} exists in the AnimatePresence block (lines 114-119 only render editor + preview). Consequently applicants-modal, applicant-{id}, applicant-status-{id}, applicant-resume-{id} testids CANNOT be reached from the UI.

ui_bugs.issue: ApplicantsModal component defined but never rendered — clicking job-applicants-{id} button opens no modal. Need to add {applicants && <ApplicantsModal job={applicants} onClose={() => setApplicants(null)} />} inside AnimatePresence around line 118.

integration_issues.issue: Full admin ATS review flow (status change, resume download) is unreachable due to modal not rendering. Only the badge count (3) is displayed on the job card.

action_items: CRITICAL: In /app/frontend/src/pages/portal/JobPostings.jsx around lines 114-119, mount ApplicantsModal conditionally: `{applicants && <ApplicantsModal job={applicants} onClose={() => setApplicants(null)} />}`. Without this, the entire admin applicant pipeline (view/status/download resume) is inaccessible from the UI even though the backend endpoints work perfectly.
critical_code_review_comments: JobPostings.jsx line 20 declares `applicants` state and line 95 wires setApplicants(j) on the job-applicants-{id} button, but the AnimatePresence block (114-119) only conditionally renders editor and preview — ApplicantsModal is imported/defined but orphaned. Fix is a one-liner addition.

careers.py apply_job (line 289) uses base64.b64decode WITHOUT validate=True — so 'invalid' base64 with skip characters may silently decode to garbage and still be persisted. Recommend `b64decode(payload.resume_b64, validate=True)` or size/format sanity check.

careers.py apply_job stores resume_b64 both on job_applications AND on the mirrored documents collection. Duplicating a 5MB blob in two collections is wasteful; consider storing once and referencing by application_id.

careers.py apply_job — the try/except around the Gmail send block is a bare `except Exception: pass` which will silently swallow real errors (e.g., token refresh failure vs network). Log the exception for observability.

careers.py get_careers_settings returns the raw doc which may leak _id since projection {'_id': 0} is applied — actually projection is correct. But default upsert branch returns dict without updated_at/updated_by keys; inconsistent shape between never-saved vs saved states.
JobPostings.jsx NotifySettings does not validate email format client-side before firing PUT — relies on server 400. Not blocking but adds a round-trip for typos.

success_rate.frontend: 70% — public apply flow works end-to-end, admin notify-settings works, but admin applicants modal completely broken (unrendered)

seed_data_creation: Backend pytest created 1 real application (test_iter77_<hex>@example.com) on react-developer via public apply, then PATCHed status→shortlisted. Frontend UI test created 1 more application (uitest_iter77_<ts>@example.com) via /careers page. Both are on the real 'react-developer' job (slug preserved). Job now has application_count=3+ visible in admin. No jobs created or deleted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Careers Mini ATS backend is fully wired: POST /api/public/careers/jobs/{slug}/apply (public, dedup by email+job, drops resume into Documents folder='Resumes', tries Gmail notify best-effort), GET /api/careers/applications?job_id=... (admin, strips resume_b64), PATCH /api/careers/applications/{id} (status enum: new/shortlisted/interview/hired/rejected), GET /api/careers/applications/{id}/resume (returns filename+mime+b64), GET/PUT /api/careers/settings (admin, notify_email). GET /api/careers/jobs includes application_count. Only remaining slug is 'react-developer'. TO RETEST: after main agent adds `{applicants && <ApplicantsModal job={applicants} onClose={() => setApplicants(null)} />}` inside AnimatePresence block of JobPostings.jsx, verify: applicants-modal opens, applicant-{id} rows list existing test applications, applicant-status-{id} dropdown changes status (persist via PATCH), applicant-resume-{id} triggers download via GET .../resume endpoint. The endpoint /careers/applications/{id}/resume is already tested and working.
rca_of_the_issue: RCA: In /app/frontend/src/pages/portal/JobPostings.jsx: (1) line 20 defines useState for `applicants`, (2) line 95 wires setApplicants(j) on button click, (3) line 179 defines ApplicantsModal component with full functionality including the correct data-testids (applicants-modal, applicant-{id}, applicant-status-{id}, applicant-resume-{id}), BUT (4) the AnimatePresence block at lines 114-119 only conditionally renders `editor` (JobEditor) and `preview` (PdfPreviewModal) — the applicants state variable is never referenced in JSX. This is a copy-paste-forget bug. REPRO: login admin → /app/website-config?tab=jobs → click applicant badge button on any job card → nothing happens (no modal opens, no console error). MITIGATION: Add one line inside AnimatePresence: `{applicants && <ApplicantsModal job={applicants} onClose={() => setApplicants(null)} />}`. All backend endpoints for this flow are verified working via pytest.
